criteria.description: Severity levels are color-coded when outputting to a TTY
criteria.evidence: search.go:132 calls output.IsTerminal(os.Stdout) to detect TTY. search.go:139 passes colorEnabled to StreamEntry. text.go:62 StreamEntry calls FormatTextColor(entry, colorEnabled). color.go:29-34 applies ansiRed for error/fatal, ansiYellow for warn/warning. 9 unit tests in color_test.go + 3 StreamEntry color tests in text_test.go confirm behavior. FormatTextColor is NOT dead code — it is the sole formatting path used by StreamEntry.

criteria.description: Colors are disabled automatically when stdout is not a TTY
criteria.evidence: tty.go:17 checks fi.Mode()&os.ModeCharDevice != 0, returning false for pipes. search.go:132 uses IsTerminal result as colorEnabled. color.go:25-27 returns plain FormatText output when colorEnabled=false. TestOU3_IsTerminalPipe confirms false for os.Pipe. TestOU3_StreamEntryColorDisabled and TestOU3_ColorDisabledNonTTY confirm no ANSI codes when disabled.

previousRejection.reason: FormatTextColor was dead code, never called from production path. No TTY detection existed.
previousRejection.resolution: StreamEntry now calls FormatTextColor instead of FormatText. IsTerminal added in tty.go. search.go wires TTY detection to StreamEntry.
